Title: Neofunctionalization of an OMT cluster dominates polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis associated with the domestication of citrus
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; citrus callus
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis / flavone O-methylation branch of flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal O-methylation steps of hydroxyflavones to PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal modifying enzyme; major control point for natural PMF variation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including DMF1, 3'-demethylnobiletin, genkwanin, cirsimaritin, lysionotin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple O-methylated PMFs including nobiletin, sinensetin, 5,7,4'-trimethyl apigenin, 8-OH-HXMF, TMF2
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosylmethionine to hydroxyl groups on flavones, preferentially at C6 and C4' and also active on C7/C3' depending on substrate, contributing to multisite O-methylation of hydroxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target PMFs and converts intermediate hydroxyflavones toward fully O-methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protective, metabolic-disorder preventive; barrier against pathogens and UV radiation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit flavedo/peel; also leaves and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in PMF-rich mandarin flavedo; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in Red tangerine flavedo relative to juice sacs and PMF-poor flavedo; expression correlates with PMF abundance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; tissue type (flavedo versus juice sac); domestication status
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; metabolome-transcriptome correlation; yeast feeding assays; recombinant enzyme kinetics; citrus callus overexpression; transient overexpression in flavedo; RNAi suppression in flavedo; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PMF accumulation; overexpression increased total PMFs by about 11.6% in flavedo, while RNAi reduced total PMFs by about 12.9%
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT3 was identified within the scaffold-4 OMT cluster linked to PMF accumulation. 2. The enzyme catalyzes multisite flavone O-methylation with preference for C6 and C4' depending on substrate. 3. Overexpression increased several PMFs in citrus callus and fruit flavedo, whereas RNAi reduced PMF levels. 4. Together with CreOMT4 and CreOMT5, it complements multisite methylation to generate citrus-specific PMFs.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA/pool-seq: BioProject PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912; metabolomics/HPLC datasets in paper and supplements
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for PMF biofortification and metabolic engineering; likely most effective when stacked with CreOMT4 and CreOMT5 rather than used alone
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regioselective but functionally complementary with CreOMT4 and CreOMT5; absent/unclonable from non-mandarin groups examined

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; citrus callus
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis / flavone O-methylation branch of flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal O-methylation steps of hydroxyflavones to PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal modifying enzyme; likely decisive control point because promoter variation strongly affects PMF accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including 3-OH-flavone, gardenin B, DMF1, lysionotin, 3'-demethylnobiletin, genkwanin, cirsimaritin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple O-methylated PMFs including nobiletin and sinensetin; converts 5-demethylnobiletin toward nobiletin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosylmethionine to hydroxyflavones at multiple positions, especially C7 and C5, and also C3, C6 and C3' depending on substrate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target PMFs and converts PMF intermediates such as 5DNOB into final PMFs such as nobiletin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protective, metabolic-disorder preventive; barrier against pathogens and UV radiation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit flavedo/peel; also leaves and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in wild and early-admixture mandarins; lower in modern cultivated mandarins; expression correlated with PMF content across development and germplasm
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in PMF-rich Red tangerine flavedo; promoter with 1,041-bp fragment confers higher expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; tissue type; domestication status; promoter structural variation with MITE-containing 1,041-bp fragment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; transcript-metabolite correlation; yeast feeding assays; recombinant enzyme kinetics; site-directed mutagenesis; citrus callus overexpression; transient flavedo overexpression; RNAi suppression; dual-luciferase promoter assay; genotyping of promoter deletion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes PMF accumulation; overexpression increased total PMFs by about 16.9%, RNAi reduced total PMFs by about 14.0%; promoter deletion lowers expression and PMF levels in cultivated mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT4 is a central member of the OMT cluster required for citrus PMF biosynthesis. 2. It catalyzes multisite O-methylation at C3, C5, C6, C7 and C3' depending on substrate and efficiently converts 5DNOB toward nobiletin. 3. N17 is essential for broad O-methylation activity, explaining functional divergence from OMT6. 4. A 1,041-bp promoter fragment containing two MITEs increases promoter activity and is associated with higher PMF content in wild mandarins. 5. Loss of this fragment in modern cultivated mandarins likely contributed to reduced PMF accumulation during domestication.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA/pool-seq: BioProject PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912; metabolomics/HPLC datasets in paper and supplements
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for marker-assisted selection using the 1,041-bp promoter fragment, and for PMF biofortification through promoter restoration/editing or gene stacking with CreOMT3 and CreOMT5
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: N17 residue is crucial for C3-, C5-, C6- and C3'-O-methylation; T168 modulates activity; promoter structural variation is a major domestication-associated determinant

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin; citrus callus
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis / flavone O-methylation branch of flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal O-methylation steps of hydroxyflavones to PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal modifying enzyme contributing to major PMF variation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including 3-OH-flavone, gardenin B, DMF1, lysionotin, 3'-demethylnobiletin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple O-methylated PMFs including nobiletin, sinensetin and related PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosylmethionine to hydroxyflavones, preferentially at C7, C5 and C3' and also active on C3/C6 depending on substrate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target PMFs and converts intermediate hydroxyflavones toward fully O-methylated PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protective, metabolic-disorder preventive; barrier against pathogens and UV radiation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit flavedo/peel; also leaves and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in PMF-rich mandarin flavedo; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in Red tangerine flavedo and correlated with PMF accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; tissue type; domestication status
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; transcript-metabolite correlation; yeast feeding assays; recombinant enzyme kinetics; citrus callus overexpression; transient flavedo overexpression; RNAi suppression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PMF accumulation; overexpression increased total PMFs by about 14.0% in flavedo, while RNAi reduced total PMFs by about 18.6%
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT5 is a third key member of the OMT cluster associated with PMF accumulation. 2. It catalyzes multisite O-methylation with high efficiency on C5, C7 and C3' and contributes to conversion of 5DNOB toward nobiletin. 3. Overexpression elevates multiple PMFs, while RNAi decreases several PMFs and total PMF content. 4. Along with CreOMT3 and CreOMT4, it complements regioselective methylation to generate seven citrus-specific PMFs.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA/pool-seq: BioProject PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912; metabolomics/HPLC datasets in paper and supplements
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful gene for PMF enhancement, especially in combination with CreOMT3 and CreOMT4 for pathway reconstruction or citrus biofortification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows neofunctionalized C3-O-methylation capability absent from ancestral-like OMT6 enzymes

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis / flavone O-methylation branch of flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Ancestral OMT branch related to PMF pathway evolution
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: terminal modification-related but weak/limited in most citrus accessions examined
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; paper states not associated with PMF content
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones; activity depends on species ortholog
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Weakly or variably O-methylated hydroxyflavone derivatives; ancestral-like reactions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: OMT6 homologs can O-methylate some hydroxyflavones, but most citrus OMT6 proteins show weak or absent activity at C3, C5 and C7; ancestral AbOMT6 methylates multiple sites
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: ancestral precursor enzyme from which the functional OMT cluster likely evolved; limited direct contribution to PMF accumulation in studied mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protective, metabolic-disorder preventive; barrier against pathogens and UV radiation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Correlated with PMF content during fruit development in Red tangerine, but generally low/negligible in species lacking PMFs or lacking OMT cluster
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low or negligible in Group I and II species; not correlated with PMF variation across broad germplasm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics and synteny; cloning of orthologs from nine accessions; recombinant protein assays; expression-metabolite correlation; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Limited or weak contribution to PMF biosynthesis in most citrus; likely ancestral progenitor of the neofunctionalized OMT cluster rather than major determinant of PMF accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT6 is highly homologous to the OMT cluster and likely represents the ancestral gene from which CreOMT3/4/5 duplicated. 2. Most OMT6 homologs showed weak or absent multisite O-methylation needed for PMF accumulation. 3. Functional divergence from CreOMT4 is largely explained by residue 17, where D17N confers broader methylation activity. 4. The paper concludes OMT6 contributed evolutionarily to the PMF pathway but is not a major current determinant of PMF variation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912; comparative genomic analyses in paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: More useful as an evolutionary template or engineering scaffold for redesigning catalytic specificity than as a direct breeding target
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper experimentally characterizes its function and evolutionary relation to PMF biosynthesis, although it is not the principal locus controlling PMF accumulation in modern mandarins
